methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Fuse Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by JC Btaiche, currently valued at $200M+ post-money following $78M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis incorporates a comparable universe of 11 peers across nuclear energy—3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to Series B transitions. Applied to the last post-money valuation of $200M, this yields an implied value of $400M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy subsector—while 7 same-sector peers exist, only 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, and 2 similar-tier companies were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical validity.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by single-method applicability, supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a median of $400M. This range reflects moderate upside from the current $200M post-money valuation. Key uncertainties include the limited peer dataset in nuclear energy at this stage, technology de-risking milestones, and regulatory pathway clarity. The analysis is anchored to one primary method; additional comparable transactions or revenue-based metrics would strengthen conviction.
